methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

private.name: Tenna Systems

data.narrative:
Theseus is a Seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector, having raised $4.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 10 peers—2 public and 8 private—spanning same-sector companies at Seed and Series A stages, including Tenna Systems ($60M), Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Stark Defence ($500M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression, using total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implies an $11M valuation. Peer-Median analysis across the 5-company comp set yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. The dispersion reflects significant heterogeneity in Defense & Security company scaling trajectories at adjacent stages.

Triangulating these two methods produces a low estimate of $9M, a median of $80M, and a high of $172M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty inherent in valuing early-stage companies in this sector. The Stage-Progression method grounds the lower bound using conservative assumptions; the Peer-Median approach anchors the upper bound but conflates Seed and Series A comparables. Key uncertainties include the trajectory and timing of Theseus's product-market fit relative to peer benchmarks, and whether management experience (CEO Stanford mechanical engineering, two CTOs with advanced CS credentials) can sustain the execution required to approach Series A peer valuations.
